brief: Specify how a card payment actually moves — authorisation, clearing, settlement — documenting every integration wire from tap to merchant payout as an engineering sheet for a payments platform team.

compose.rationale: Selected 'dgm-blueprint' (deck-dgm-blueprint, art-directed style); shares audience(s) [technical, risk-and-governance]; matched intent keyword(s) [document, system, rails, specify, integration]; art-directed style fits expressive corporate suitability; motion preference 2 noted (the template locks its own motion).

sections.purpose: The tour cover — deck identity, thesis standfirst, and the provenance notice.

spec.name: deck.code
spec.guidance: Short deck code shown in the chrome, e.g. "DGM-01".

slots.guidance: Short deck code shown in the chrome, e.g. "DGM-01".

spec.name: deck.world
spec.guidance: The world name paired with the code in the chrome.

slots.guidance: The world name paired with the code in the chrome.

spec.name: deck.title
spec.guidance: The deck title set large on the cover.

slots.guidance: The deck title set large on the cover.

spec.name: deck.standfirst
spec.guidance: One paragraph framing the whole story the eight diagrams tell.

slots.guidance: One paragraph framing the whole story the eight diagrams tell.

spec.name: deck.notice
spec.guidance: REQUIRED data-provenance notice (synthetic or sourced), shown in the deck chrome.

slots.guidance: REQUIRED data-provenance notice (synthetic or sourced), shown in the deck chrome.

sections.purpose: The close — takeaways and the sign-off ask.

spec.name: close.takeaways
spec.guidance: Three-to-six one-line takeaways, each earned by a diagram in the deck.

slots.guidance: Three-to-six one-line takeaways, each earned by a diagram in the deck.

spec.name: close.signoff

slots.guidance: One paragraph: what the reader should do or decide next.

craftGuarantees: The deck is a grammar tour: cover, then EIGHT diagram slides (flow, sequence, layers, zones, cycle, compare, cells, timeline), then the close. Author every slide from one coherent topic so the deck reads as one story, not eight samples.

Diagram fields embed the diagram grammar directly: node/edge/item ids must cross-resolve (flow edges → node ids; messages → actor ids; zone links → zone-node ids; compare rows match column count; nowIndex inside eras).
Headings are claims, captions are the reading instructions, the diagram is the proof. Never caption a diagram with a restatement of its title.
deck.notice must state data provenance (synthetic or sourced) — it renders in the chrome on every slide.
timeline.nowIndex is optional in the schema but strongly encouraged: flag where the story stands today.
